question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked the user zero didactic questions. It wrote the full solution and explained it, never engaging the user at any decision point (e.g., negative-shift handling, case preservation strategy, decode-as-negated-encode).

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all. The two-option didactic format the project is built around was completely bypassed — no forks were surfaced, consequential or otherwise.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolding because there are no questions. The Pair delivered the answer wholesale, including the 'key insight' about `% 26` handling negatives, which would have been the ideal teachable moment to elicit.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the explanation is clear, but the conversation is one-shot monologue with no back-and-forth. The user's Turn 2 message is itself just a recap, indicating no real dialogue occurred.

effectiveness.rationale: The code is correct and equivalent to the baseline (same algorithm, same edge-case handling). Note the Pair claimed it 'can't write to disk' despite a Write tool call shown in the transcript, but the resulting code is functionally identical to baseline.

friction.rationale: Slightly longer than baseline (45s vs 33s, one extra turn of recap) with no pedagogical payoff to justify the extra length. Roughly comparable in efficiency.
